ref: He then got into the boat with them, and the wind died down. The disciples were completely confused.
text: Gee nyaa zom uureŋɔ la zuo la ba, ti kusebego la ma'aɛ, ti la iŋɛ ba za'a pakerɛ.
translated: The chief of the village has gone to the farm, and the elder has gone to the market, and they left the children playing.

ref: Large crowds came and brought many people who were paralyzed or blind or lame or unable to talk. They placed them, and many others, in front of Jesus, and he healed them all.
text: Ti nɛrekuuŋɔ kina a zi'an na, tara wɔbehi, kɔɔrenɔ, fɔɔhi, gɔɔnɔ, la basɛba zo'e zo'e, tari ba na gã'arɛ Yezu nɛŋan, ti a me tibɛ ba.
translated: They brought all kinds of animals, including cattle, sheep, goats, fowl, and all sorts of creatures, and presented them to Jesus' disciples, who gave them to the people.

ref: That evening, Jesus said to his disciples, “Let's cross to the east side.”
text: De daarɛ mɛa Zaanuurɛ, ti a yele a zahereba la yeti, “Bahɛ ya ti tu yakɛ ko'om wa yakenɛ deyima wa ha.”
translated: The elder of Zaare said to the young men, "Come, let us go and fetch water and bring it to our homes."

ref: Someone may say, “Here is the Messiah!” or “There he is!” But don't believe it.
text: Bala la, nɛra han ta yele fum yeti ‘Bihɛ, Kiresta n ŋwana,’ bii a boi la ke. De nam de.
translated: Come and see, the person is saying that Jesus is the Son of God, the thing is true.

ref: Jesus replied, “It isn't right to take food away from children and feed it to dogs.”
text: Ti Yezu lerege en yeti, “La dagi ma'a ti n dikɛ kɔma dia lobe bo' baahi.”
translated: Jesus looked at them and said, "It is written: 'Man shall not live on bread alone."
